message:
feat(packs): add storage.minio and storage.azure_blob packs

- storage.minio: MinIO Client (mc) pack with 14 safe and 6 destructive patterns
  - Covers bucket/object operations, admin commands, mirror with remove
- storage.azure_blob: Azure Blob Storage pack with 19 safe and 6 destructive patterns
  - Covers az storage CLI and azcopy operations
  - Handles container/blob deletion, account deletion, sync --delete-destination

All storage packs now complete: s3, gcs, minio, azure_blob

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","title":"Performance budgets + benchmark enforcement","description":"## Objective

Codify performance budgets and enforce them in CI so `dcg` stays invisible.

## Budgets to encode

- Fast path (no heredoc/inline): p99 under 500us
- Slow path (heredoc/inline): p99 under 20ms
- Absolute max: fail-open beyond 50ms

These numbers can be revised, but must be explicit.

## Requirements

- Benchmarks for:
  - quick reject / keyword gating
  - normalization
  - pack evaluation
  - overrides/allowlist
  - heredoc trigger/extract/AST path
- A CI job that compares benchmarks to budgets (tolerant of variance).

## Acceptance criteria

- Budgets exist as code constants/config.
- CI can detect large regressions and fail.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.5","title":"Regression corpus harness (true/false positives + bypass attempts)","description":"## Objective

Create and maintain a small, curated corpus of high-signal command cases and a harness that runs them through:

- hook JSON path
- CLI evaluator path

## Corpus categories

- `true_positives/` (must block)
- `false_positives/` (must allow)
- `bypass_attempts/` (must block or warn)
- `edge_cases/` (must not crash/hang)

## Output/logging requirements

- Harness prints per-case:
  - description
  - expected decision
  - actual decision
  - rule id when matched

## Acceptance criteria

- Corpus harness runs in CI.
- Adding a new regression is easy and does not require writing Rust test code each time.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7zg","title":"Add UBS installation and run steps to CI workflow","description":"# Task: Add UBS Installation and Run Steps to CI

## What to Do

Add two steps to the check job in .github/workflows/ci.yml:
1. Install UBS using the official installer
2. Run UBS on changed Rust files

## CI Steps to Add

After the clippy step, add:

```yaml
- name: Install UBS
  run: |
    curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner/master/install.sh" \
      | bash -s -- --easy-mode
    # Add UBS to PATH for this job
    echo "$HOME/.ubs" >> $GITHUB_PATH

- name: Run UBS on changed files
  if: always()
  run: |
    echo "=== UBS Static Analysis ===" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    echo "Timestamp: $(date -Iseconds)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    
    # Get changed Rust files
    if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
      CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD -- "*.rs" | tr "\n" " ")
      echo "Mode: Pull Request (base: ${{ github.base_ref }})" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    else
      CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1 -- "*.rs" | tr "\n" " ")
      echo "Mode: Push (comparing to HEAD~1)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    fi
    
    echo "Changed files: ${CHANGED:-none}" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    
    if [ -n "$CHANGED" ]; then
      echo "Running UBS analysis..." | tee -a ubs-output.log
      # Run UBS with verbose output
      if ubs --verbose $CHANGED 2>&1 | tee -a ubs-output.log; then
        echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
        echo "UBS Result: PASSED (no issues found)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
      else
        echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
        echo "UBS Result: WARNINGS (issues found - non-blocking)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
        echo "::warning::UBS found potential issues in changed files"
      fi
    else
      echo "UBS Result: SKIPPED (no Rust files changed)" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    fi
    
    # Summary
    echo "" | tee -a ubs-output.log
    echo "=== End UBS Analysis ===" | tee -a ubs-output.log

- name: UBS summary
  if: always()
  run: |
    echo "## UBS Static Analysis" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
    echo "\`\`\`" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
    cat ubs-output.log >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY 2>/dev/null || echo "No UBS output" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
    echo "\`\`\`" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

- name: Upload UBS output
  if: failure()
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: ubs-output
    path: ubs-output.log
    retention-days: 7
```

## Key Fixes from Original

1. **Binary Path**: Added `echo "$HOME/.ubs" >> $GITHUB_PATH` to make ubs available
2. **Detailed Logging**: All output goes to ubs-output.log with timestamps
3. **Clear Status**: Explicit PASSED/WARNINGS/SKIPPED messages
4. **Artifact Upload**: Save output on failure for debugging
5. **Step Summary**: UBS results appear in GitHub Actions summary

## Why --verbose

The verbose flag provides:
- File-by-file analysis progress
- Detailed issue descriptions
- Context around found issues

## Why Warning Mode

Using warning annotations instead of failing because:
- UBS is new to this repo, may have false positives
- Dont want to block PRs unexpectedly
- Can change to blocking after tuning

## Files to Modify

- .github/workflows/ci.yml (check job)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] UBS install step added with PATH setup
- [ ] UBS run step added with detailed logging
- [ ] Changed-files logic works for PRs and pushes
- [ ] ubs-output.log captures all analysis output
- [ ] Step summary shows UBS results
- [ ] Artifacts uploaded on failure
- [ ] CI passes with new steps","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- CI must capture UBS install/run logs and upload them as artifacts on failure.
- Add a small validation step that ensures the UBS command executes on a known safe file and reports zero findings (logged).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:23:27.284229987-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:43:05.045123537-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:43:05.045123537-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","title":"[TASK] Performance regression testing for packs","description":"# Performance Regression Testing for Packs

## Scope
Ensure adding new packs doesn't degrade dcg performance.

## Deliverables

### 1. Performance Benchmark Suite
Create `benches/pack_performance.rs`:
```rust
use criterion::{criterion_group, Criterion};

fn bench_single_pack_evaluation(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark each pack individually
}

fn bench_all_packs_evaluation(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark with all packs enabled
}

fn bench_pattern_matching(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark regex pattern matching specifically
}
```

### 2. Performance Budgets
Define maximum acceptable times:
- Single pack evaluation: < 500µs
- All packs evaluation: < 5ms
- Regex pattern match: < 100µs per pattern
- Total hook execution: < 10ms

### 3. CI Performance Tracking
- Run benchmarks on every PR
- Compare against baseline
- Fail PR if regression > 10%
- Track performance over time (grafana/dashboard)

### 4. Performance Logging
When DEBUG enabled:
- Log time per pack
- Log time per pattern
- Identify slow patterns
- Suggest optimizations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Benchmark suite covers all performance-critical paths
- [ ] Budgets defined and enforced in CI
- [ ] Performance tracked over time
- [ ] Slow patterns flagged for optimization","notes":"Context (perf reality check):
- Current process-per-command baseline (release) is ~173–179ms p50 for normal hook invocations because pack registry initialization compiles regexes.
- Criterion benches (in-process) are still useful, but they do NOT capture process startup + per-invocation regex compilation cost.

Plan adjustments:
- Treat `git_safety_guard-64dc` (lazy registry + lazy compilation) as the prerequisite that makes pack performance budgets meaningful.
- After startup costs are eliminated, criterion benches can focus on steady-state evaluation cost (sanitize/normalize/pack matching).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rbst","title":"Implement robustness and edge case handling for heredoc parsing","description":"# Robustness and Edge Case Handling for Heredoc/Inline Script Analysis

## Objective

Ensure heredoc detection + extraction + AST matching are *robust by construction*:

- never panic
- never hang
- never allocate unbounded memory
- fail-open on malformed input/timeouts (hook safety)

This task focuses on **edge cases** and **hardening** (the stuff that only shows up in the wild).

## Background

Heredoc and inline-script analysis is the highest-risk path for stability because inputs are:

- multiline
- potentially huge
- often malformed (especially from agents)
- can include confusing quoting/indentation variants

A single crash/hang in this path makes users disable dcg.

## Hardening requirements (v1)

### 1) Size limits (must be enforced)

- enforce a max extracted body size and max line count
- limits must be configurable (see global limit work) and *never* exceed sane defaults

Behavior:

- if limits are exceeded: **skip deep analysis** and fail-open
- record a structured log/event when this happens (so it is debuggable)

### 2) Deadline / timeout protection (must be enforced)

- tier 2 extraction must be bounded
- tier 3 AST parsing/matching must be bounded
- if using external processes (ast-grep), they must have a hard timeout + kill

Behavior:

- on timeout: skip deep analysis, fail-open, log `skipped_due_to_budget`

### 3) Malformed heredoc handling (must be graceful)

Handle without panic:

- missing delimiter
- unterminated heredoc
- invalid delimiter tokens
- invalid indentation stripping cases (`<<-`)
- multiple heredocs in a single command

### 4) Non-UTF8 / binary-ish content (must be safe)

- detect binary-like payloads (null bytes, high non-printable ratio)
- do not feed binary-ish input into AST parsing

Behavior:

- skip analysis and fail-open; log `skipped_binary_content`

### 5) Multiple heredocs / nested triggers (bounded)

- support multiple heredocs per command up to a small cap
- if nested heredoc triggers appear inside a body:
  - treat as **low-confidence** signal initially (WARN/log), not auto-DENY
  - avoid recursion in v1; recursion can be a separate follow-up if needed

## Observability requirements

When deep analysis is skipped, the system must surface why (in trace/log):

- exceeded size limits
- deadline/budget exceeded
- parse error / malformed input
- binary-ish content detected

## Acceptance criteria

- No panics for arbitrary inputs (validated via fuzz/proptest targets).
- Extraction and analysis never exceed configured limits/budgets.
- Skips are visible in `dcg explain` and structured logs.
- Multiple heredocs are handled up to the configured cap.

## Tests

- Unit tests:
  - oversized heredoc → skip + fail-open + trace/log marker
  - unterminated heredoc → skip + fail-open
  - binary-ish heredoc → skip + fail-open
  - multiple heredocs parsed up to cap

- Fuzzing:
  - heredoc extraction fuzz target must find no panics

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-99e.10` global size limits (single source of truth)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.14` evaluation time budget/deadline semantics
- `git_safety_guard-y4j` extraction implementation
- `git_safety_guard-tlog` logging/trace integration for heredoc path

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rl8","title":"Enforce coverage thresholds in CI (llvm-cov)","description":"## Objective

Turn coverage reporting from informational to **enforced**, with documented thresholds and failure behavior.

## Scope

- Use `cargo llvm-cov` output already produced in CI.
- Define initial minimum thresholds (global + key modules).
- Fail CI when thresholds are not met.

## Notes

- Thresholds should be set **after** baseline audit (see dependencies).
- Keep thresholds modest at first, with a follow-up plan to ratchet.

## Acceptance criteria

- CI fails when coverage drops below defined thresholds.
- Coverage summary still attached as artifact / job summary.
- Thresholds documented in the repo (doc or CI comments).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-rm2n","title":"[TASK] User experience for pack discovery and feedback","description":"# User Experience for Pack Discovery and Feedback

## Scope
Ensure users can easily discover, configure, and provide feedback on packs.

## Deliverables

### 1. Pack Discovery CLI Commands
Implement new dcg commands:
```bash
# List all available packs
dcg packs list

# Show details about a specific pack
dcg packs info secrets.vault

# Show which packs would match a command
dcg explain 'vault secrets disable foo'

# Show packs enabled in current config
dcg packs enabled
```

### 2. Pack Configuration UX
Make configuration intuitive:
```toml
# .dcg.toml example with clear documentation
[packs]
# Enable all Tier 1 packs (critical security)
tier1 = true

# Selectively enable Tier 2 packs
enable = ["dns.cloudflare", "monitoring.datadog"]

# Disable specific packs if needed  
disable = ["backup.rclone"]  # We use custom backup scripts
```

### 3. Helpful Error Messages
When a command is blocked:
```
⚠️  BLOCKED: vault secrets disable production-secrets

Pack: secrets.vault
Reason: 'vault secrets disable' permanently disables a secrets engine,
        potentially causing data loss for all secrets stored there.

This looks intentional? You can:
  1. Add to allowlist: dcg allow 'vault secrets disable production-secrets'
  2. Run with bypass: DCG_BYPASS=1 vault secrets disable production-secrets
  3. Report false positive: https://github.com/.../issues/new?template=false_positive

Docs: https://dcg.dev/packs/secrets/vault
```

### 4. Feedback Collection
- GitHub issue templates for:
  - False positive reports
  - False negative reports  
  - New pack requests
- Telemetry (opt-in) for pattern match statistics
- User survey mechanism

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pack list/info commands implemented
- [ ] Configuration is well-documented
- [ ] Error messages are helpful and actionable

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-u5w","title":"Create codecov.yml configuration file","description":"# Task: Create codecov.yml Configuration File

## What to Do

Create a `codecov.yml` file in the repository root to configure Codecov's behavior for PR comments, coverage targets, and file ignores.

## Configuration Content

```yaml
# codecov.yml - Codecov configuration for DCG
# Docs: https://docs.codecov.com/docs/codecov-yaml

codecov:
  require_ci_to_pass: true

coverage:
  precision: 2
  round: down
  range: "60...100"
  
  status:
    project:
      default:
        # Match our CI-enforced threshold
        target: 70%
        threshold: 2%  # Allow 2% fluctuation without failing
        
    patch:
      default:
        # New code should have high coverage
        target: 80%
        threshold: 5%

comment:
  layout: "reach,diff,flags,tree,betaprofiling"
  behavior: default
  require_changes: true
  require_base: false
  require_head: true

ignore:
  - "tests/**"
  - "benches/**"
  - "fuzz/**"
  - "*.md"
  - "scripts/**"

flags:
  unittests:
    paths:
      - src/
    carryforward: true
```

## Configuration Explained

### Coverage Status
- **project target: 70%** - Matches our CI-enforced minimum
- **patch target: 80%** - New code in PRs should be well-tested
- **threshold: 2%** - Small fluctuations don't fail status checks

### Comment Layout
- **reach** - Shows lines covered
- **diff** - Shows coverage diff vs base
- **flags** - Shows coverage by flag (unittests)
- **tree** - Shows file-by-file breakdown
- **betaprofiling** - Shows performance impact (beta feature)

### Ignore Patterns
We exclude test/bench/fuzz directories since:
- Test code doesn't need coverage (it IS the coverage)
- Benchmark code is performance testing, not unit testing
- Fuzz targets are input generators

### Flags
The `unittests` flag lets us track unit test coverage separately from integration tests if we add more test suites later.

## Why These Settings

1. **Targets match CI** - Codecov targets align with our enforced thresholds
2. **PR comments enabled** - Developers see coverage impact immediately
3. **Ignores match llvm-cov** - Same exclusions as our cargo llvm-cov config
4. **Carryforward** - If tests don't run, use previous coverage data

## Files to Create

- `codecov.yml` (in repo root)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] codecov.yml created in repo root
- [ ] Project target set to 70%
- [ ] Patch target set to 80%
- [ ] Ignore patterns match our llvm-cov config

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-uau","title":"Define Bash/Shell destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"## Objective

Define how to detect destructive shell operations inside *shell* heredoc bodies with high signal and low false positives.

## Key insight

For shell heredocs, we should avoid inventing a second “pattern language” if we can reuse the existing dcg evaluator.

Preferred approach:

1. Parse the heredoc body as shell (tree-sitter-bash).
2. Extract candidate simple-command invocations (command + args).
3. Reconstruct a normalized command string.
4. Feed each extracted command into the existing evaluator (packs + overrides + severity policy).

This keeps all destructive knowledge in one place (packs) and reduces drift.

## What to extract (v1)

- Simple commands: `rm -rf ...`, `git reset --hard ...`, `docker system prune`, etc.
- Pipe targets: right-hand side of `|` (executed)
- Command substitutions: `$(...)` / backticks (executed)

## What NOT to treat as executed (avoid false positives)

- comments
- string literals printed with `echo`/`printf`
- here-doc delimiters themselves

## Severity guidance

- Direct matches to catastrophic pack rules → `Error` (deny by default)
- Ambiguous/dynamic constructs (variable-built commands) → `Warning` at most unless payload is confidently destructive

## Testing requirements

- Positive fixtures (must detect):
  - `rm -rf /` (or other catastrophic)
  - `git reset --hard`
  - `git clean -fd`
  - `docker system prune` (if pack enabled)
- Negative fixtures (must not flag):
  - `echo "rm -rf"`
  - comments mentioning dangerous commands
  - harmless cleanup under `/tmp` (if policy allows)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines the extraction→evaluation approach clearly enough to implement without re-reading external plans.
- Produces a fixture list that covers:
  - executed vs data-only contexts
  - pipes and command substitution
- Avoids introducing new destructive-rule definitions for shell when existing packs already encode them.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-unit","title":"Create comprehensive unit test suite for heredoc detection","description":"# Unit Test Suite for Heredoc Detection

## Objective

Create thorough unit tests for each component of the heredoc detection system.

## Test Modules

### 1. Tier 1 Regex Tests (`tests/tier1_regex.rs`)

```rust
mod tier1_regex_tests {
    use super::*;
    
    #[test]
    fn test_bash_heredoc_variants() {
        // Standard heredoc
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat << EOF"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat <<EOF"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat << 'EOF'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat << \"EOF\""));
        
        // Indented heredoc
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat <<- EOF"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat <<-EOF"));
        
        // Here-string
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat <<< 'hello'"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_inline_script_flags() {
        // Python
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("python -c 'print(1)'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("python3 -c 'print(1)'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("python -e 'print(1)'")); // -e not valid but we catch
        
        // Node.js
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("node -e 'console.log(1)'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("node -p 'process.version'"));
        
        // Ruby
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("ruby -e 'puts 1'"));
        
        // Perl
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("perl -e 'print 1'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("perl -E 'say 1'"));
        
        // Shell
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("bash -c 'echo hi'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("sh -c 'echo hi'"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("zsh -c 'echo hi'"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_pipe_to_interpreter() {
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("curl http://x.com | bash"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("cat script.py | python3"));
        assert!(might_contain_heredoc("echo code | node"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_negative_cases() {
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("git status"));
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("cargo build"));
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("ls -la"));
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("cat file.txt"));
        assert!(!might_contain_heredoc("grep pattern file"));
    }
}
```

### 2. Heredoc Extraction Tests (`tests/extraction.rs`)

```rust
mod extraction_tests {
    #[test]
    fn test_extract_simple_heredoc() {
        let cmd = "python3 << 'EOF'\nprint('hello')\nEOF";
        let heredoc = extract_heredoc(cmd).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(heredoc.content, "print('hello')\n");
        assert_eq!(heredoc.delimiter, "EOF");
        assert!(heredoc.quoted);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_extract_indented_heredoc() {
        let cmd = "bash <<- 'END'\n\techo hello\n\techo world\n\tEND";
        let heredoc = extract_heredoc(cmd).unwrap();
        // <<- strips leading tabs
        assert_eq!(heredoc.content, "echo hello\necho world\n");
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_extract_inline_script() {
        let cmd = "python3 -c 'import os; os.system(\"ls\")'";
        let heredoc = extract_heredoc(cmd).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(heredoc.content, "import os; os.system(\"ls\")");
    }
}
```

### 3. Language Detection Tests (`tests/language_detection.rs`)

```rust
mod language_detection_tests {
    #[test]
    fn test_detect_from_command() {
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("python3 -c"), Language::Python);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("node -e"), Language::JavaScript);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("ruby -e"), Language::Ruby);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("perl -e"), Language::Perl);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_cmd("bash -c"), Language::Bash);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_detect_from_shebang() {
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("#!/usr/bin/env python3\n"), Language::Python);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("#!/bin/bash\n"), Language::Bash);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("#!/usr/bin/node\n"), Language::JavaScript);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_detect_from_content_heuristics() {
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("import os\n"), Language::Python);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("require('fs')\n"), Language::JavaScript);
        assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("use strict;\n"), Language::Perl);
    }
}
```

### 4. Pattern Matching Tests (`tests/patterns.rs`)

One submodule per language with comprehensive tests.

### 5. False Positive Tests (`tests/false_positives.rs`)

    }

+//! Azure Blob Storage pack - protections for destructive Azure storage operations.
+//!
+//! Covers destructive operations:
+//! - Container deletion (az storage container delete)
+//! - Blob deletion (az storage blob delete, delete-batch)
+//! - Storage account deletion
+//! - azcopy remove and sync --delete-destination
+
+use crate::packs::{DestructivePattern, Pack, SafePattern};
+use crate::{destructive_pattern, safe_pattern};
+
+/// Create the Azure Blob Storage pack.
+#[must_use]
+pub fn create_pack() -> Pack {
+    Pack {
+        id: "storage.azure_blob".to_string(),
+        name: "Azure Blob Storage",
+        description: "Protects against destructive Azure Blob Storage operations like container \
+                      deletion, blob deletion, and azcopy remove.",
+        keywords: &["az storage", "azcopy"],
+        safe_patterns: create_safe_patterns(),
+        destructive_patterns: create_destructive_patterns(),
+        keyword_matcher: None,
+    }
+}
+
+fn create_safe_patterns() -> Vec<SafePattern> {
+    vec![
+        // az storage container operations (read-only)
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-container-list",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+container\s+list\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-container-show",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+container\s+show\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-container-exists",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+container\s+exists\b"
+        ),
+        // az storage blob operations (read-only)
+        safe_pattern!("az-storage-blob-list", r"\baz\s+storage\s+blob\s+list\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("az-storage-blob-show", r"\baz\s+storage\s+blob\s+show\b"),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-blob-exists",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+blob\s+exists\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-blob-download",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+blob\s+download\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-blob-download-batch",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+blob\s+download-batch\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-blob-url",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+blob\s+url\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-blob-metadata-show",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+blob\s+metadata\s+show\b"
+        ),
+        // az storage account operations (read-only)
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-account-list",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+account\s+list\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-account-show",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+account\s+show\b"
+        ),
+        safe_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-account-keys-list",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+account\s+keys\s+list\b"
+        ),
+        // azcopy operations (read-only)
+        safe_pattern!("azcopy-list", r"\bazcopy\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*list\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("azcopy-copy", r"\bazcopy\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*copy\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("azcopy-jobs-list", r"\bazcopy\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*jobs\s+list\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("azcopy-jobs-show", r"\bazcopy\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*jobs\s+show\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("azcopy-login", r"\bazcopy\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*login\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("azcopy-env", r"\bazcopy\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*env\b"),
+    ]
+}
+
+fn create_destructive_patterns() -> Vec<DestructivePattern> {
+    vec![
+        // Container deletion
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-container-delete",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+container\s+delete\b",
+            "az storage container delete removes an Azure storage container."
+        ),
+        // Blob deletion (order matters: delete-batch before delete)
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-blob-delete-batch",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+blob\s+delete-batch\b",
+            "az storage blob delete-batch removes multiple blobs from Azure storage."
+        ),
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-blob-delete",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+blob\s+delete\b(?!-)",
+            "az storage blob delete removes a blob from Azure storage."
+        ),
+        // Storage account deletion
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "az-storage-account-delete",
+            r"\baz\s+storage\s+account\s+delete\b",
+            "az storage account delete removes an entire Azure storage account."
+        ),
+        // azcopy remove
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "azcopy-remove",
+            r"\bazcopy\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*remove\b",
+            "azcopy remove deletes files from Azure storage."
+        ),
+        // azcopy sync with delete
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "azcopy-sync-delete",
+            r"\bazcopy\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*sync\b.*--delete-destination\b",
+            "azcopy sync --delete-destination removes destination files not in source."
+        ),
+    ]
+}
+
+#[cfg(test)]
+mod tests {
+    use super::*;
+    use crate::packs::test_helpers::*;
+
+    #[test]
+    fn test_pack_creation() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        assert_eq!(pack.id, "storage.azure_blob");
+        assert_eq!(pack.name, "Azure Blob Storage");
+        assert!(!pack.description.is_empty());
+        assert!(pack.keywords.contains(&"az storage"));
+        assert!(pack.keywords.contains(&"azcopy"));
+
+        assert_patterns_compile(&pack);
+        assert_all_patterns_have_reasons(&pack);
+        assert_unique_pattern_names(&pack);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn allows_safe_commands() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        // Container operations
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage container list --account-name myaccount");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage container show -n mycontainer");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage container exists -n mycontainer");
+        // Blob operations
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage blob list -c mycontainer");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage blob show -c mycontainer -n myblob");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage blob exists -c mycontainer -n myblob");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage blob download -c mycontainer -n myblob -f local.txt",
+        );
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage blob download-batch -d ./local -s mycontainer",
+        );
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage blob url -c mycontainer -n myblob");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage blob metadata show -c mycontainer -n myblob",
+        );
+        // Account operations
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage account list");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage account show -n myaccount");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "az storage account keys list -n myaccount");
+        // azcopy operations
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "azcopy list https://account.blob.core.windows.net/");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(
+            &pack,
+            "azcopy copy ./local https://account.blob.core.windows.net/container",
+        );
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "azcopy jobs list");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "azcopy jobs show jobid");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "azcopy login");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "azcopy env");
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn blocks_destructive_commands() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        // Container deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage container delete -n mycontainer",
+            "az-storage-container-delete",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage container delete --name mycontainer --account-name myaccount",
+            "az-storage-container-delete",
+        );
+        // Blob deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage blob delete -c mycontainer -n myblob",
+            "az-storage-blob-delete",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage blob delete-batch -s mycontainer",
+            "az-storage-blob-delete-batch",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage blob delete-batch --source mycontainer --pattern '*.log'",
+            "az-storage-blob-delete-batch",
+        );
+        // Account deletion
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage account delete -n myaccount",
+            "az-storage-account-delete",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "az storage account delete --name myaccount --yes",
+            "az-storage-account-delete",
+        );
+        // azcopy remove
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "azcopy remove https://account.blob.core.windows.net/container/blob",
+            "azcopy-remove",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "azcopy remove --recursive https://account.blob.core.windows.net/container",
+            "azcopy-remove",
+        );
+        // azcopy sync with delete
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "azcopy sync ./local https://account.blob.core.windows.net/container --delete-destination true",
+            "azcopy-sync-delete",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "azcopy sync --delete-destination=true ./src https://dest.blob.core.windows.net/",
+            "azcopy-sync-delete",
+        );
+    }
+}

+//! MinIO pack - protections for destructive MinIO Client (mc) operations.
+//!
+//! Covers destructive operations:
+//! - Bucket removal (mc rb)
+//! - Object deletion (mc rm)
+//! - Admin bucket deletion
+//! - Mirror with remove
+
+use crate::packs::{DestructivePattern, Pack, SafePattern};
+use crate::{destructive_pattern, safe_pattern};
+
+/// Create the MinIO pack.
+#[must_use]
+pub fn create_pack() -> Pack {
+    Pack {
+        id: "storage.minio".to_string(),
+        name: "MinIO",
+        description: "Protects against destructive MinIO Client (mc) operations like bucket \
+                      removal, object deletion, and admin operations.",
+        keywords: &["mc"],
+        safe_patterns: create_safe_patterns(),
+        destructive_patterns: create_destructive_patterns(),
+        keyword_matcher: None,
+    }
+}
+
+fn create_safe_patterns() -> Vec<SafePattern> {
+    vec![
+        // List operations
+        safe_pattern!("mc-ls", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*ls\b"),
+        // Read operations
+        safe_pattern!("mc-cat", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*cat\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("mc-head", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*head\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("mc-stat", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*stat\b"),
+        // Copy operations (non-destructive)
+        safe_pattern!("mc-cp", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*cp\b"),
+        // Diff/compare
+        safe_pattern!("mc-diff", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*diff\b"),
+        // Find
+        safe_pattern!("mc-find", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*find\b"),
+        // Disk usage
+        safe_pattern!("mc-du", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*du\b"),
+        // Version/help
+        safe_pattern!("mc-version", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*version\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("mc-help", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*(?:--help|-h)\b"),
+        // Admin info (read-only)
+        safe_pattern!("mc-admin-info", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*admin\s+info\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("mc-admin-user-list", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*admin\s+user\s+list\b"),
+        safe_pattern!("mc-admin-policy-list", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*admin\s+policy\s+list\b"),
+        // Alias management (config, not data)
+        safe_pattern!("mc-alias-list", r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*alias\s+list\b"),
+    ]
+}
+
+fn create_destructive_patterns() -> Vec<DestructivePattern> {
+    vec![
+        // Bucket removal
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "mc-rb",
+            r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*rb\b",
+            "mc rb removes a MinIO bucket."
+        ),
+        // Object removal
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "mc-rm",
+            r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*rm\b",
+            "mc rm deletes objects from MinIO."
+        ),
+        // Admin bucket delete
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "mc-admin-bucket-delete",
+            r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*admin\s+bucket\s+(?:delete|remove)\b",
+            "mc admin bucket delete removes a bucket via admin API."
+        ),
+        // Mirror with remove (sync with delete)
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "mc-mirror-remove",
+            r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*mirror\b.*--remove\b",
+            "mc mirror --remove deletes destination objects not in source."
+        ),
+        // Admin user remove
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "mc-admin-user-remove",
+            r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*admin\s+user\s+(?:remove|disable)\b",
+            "mc admin user remove/disable affects user access."
+        ),
+        // Admin policy remove
+        destructive_pattern!(
+            "mc-admin-policy-remove",
+            r"\bmc\s+(?:--\S+\s+)*admin\s+policy\s+(?:remove|unset)\b",
+            "mc admin policy remove/unset modifies access policies."
+        ),
+    ]
+}
+
+#[cfg(test)]
+mod tests {
+    use super::*;
+    use crate::packs::test_helpers::*;
+
+    #[test]
+    fn test_pack_creation() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        assert_eq!(pack.id, "storage.minio");
+        assert_eq!(pack.name, "MinIO");
+        assert!(!pack.description.is_empty());
+        assert!(pack.keywords.contains(&"mc"));
+
+        assert_patterns_compile(&pack);
+        assert_all_patterns_have_reasons(&pack);
+        assert_unique_pattern_names(&pack);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn allows_safe_commands() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        // List operations
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc ls myminio/bucket");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc --json ls myminio/bucket");
+        // Read operations
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc cat myminio/bucket/file.txt");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc head myminio/bucket/file.txt");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc stat myminio/bucket/file.txt");
+        // Copy operations
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc cp localfile myminio/bucket/");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc cp myminio/bucket/file.txt ./local");
+        // Diff
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc diff myminio/bucket1 myminio/bucket2");
+        // Find
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc find myminio/bucket --name '*.txt'");
+        // Disk usage
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc du myminio/bucket");
+        // Version/help
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc version");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc --help");
+        // Admin info
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc admin info myminio");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc admin user list myminio");
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc admin policy list myminio");
+        // Alias
+        assert_safe_pattern_matches(&pack, "mc alias list");
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn blocks_destructive_commands() {
+        let pack = create_pack();
+        // Bucket removal
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(&pack, "mc rb myminio/bucket", "mc-rb");
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(&pack, "mc rb --force myminio/bucket", "mc-rb");
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(&pack, "mc --json rb myminio/bucket", "mc-rb");
+        // Object removal
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(&pack, "mc rm myminio/bucket/file.txt", "mc-rm");
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(&pack, "mc rm --recursive myminio/bucket/", "mc-rm");
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(&pack, "mc rm --force myminio/bucket/file.txt", "mc-rm");
+        // Admin bucket delete
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "mc admin bucket delete myminio bucket",
+            "mc-admin-bucket-delete",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "mc admin bucket remove myminio bucket",
+            "mc-admin-bucket-delete",
+        );
+        // Mirror with remove
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "mc mirror --remove myminio/src myminio/dst",
+            "mc-mirror-remove",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "mc mirror myminio/src myminio/dst --remove",
+            "mc-mirror-remove",
+        );
+        // Admin user operations
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "mc admin user remove myminio username",
+            "mc-admin-user-remove",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "mc admin user disable myminio username",
+            "mc-admin-user-remove",
+        );
+        // Admin policy operations
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "mc admin policy remove myminio policyname",
+            "mc-admin-policy-remove",
+        );
+        assert_blocks_with_pattern(
+            &pack,
+            "mc admin policy unset myminio policyname",
+            "mc-admin-policy-remove",
+        );
+    }
+}
